---
title: "Wrap-up and Final-Exam Preparation"
---

::: {.callout-important}
## Chapter in development

This chapter is being authored (Phase C of the production plan).
It will review the full reproducible pipeline and walk through a
complete end-to-end case study on one open course dataset, used
as both review and final-exam practice. The structure below is
the outline; prose and worked code are coming.
:::

# Chapter goals {.unnumbered}

By the end of this chapter you will be able to:

- Trace the full reproducible workflow taught in this book —
  **import → clean → summarize → reshape → join → visualize →
  map → render → explain** — on a single forestry dataset.
- Reproduce a complete analysis from a clean project, from raw
  file to rendered Quarto report.
- Prepare for the final exam (R, tidyverse, ggplot2, and spatial
  mapping).

# Expected output for this chapter {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip}
## What you will produce

A complete **end-to-end case study walkthrough** on any one of
the course datasets: start from the raw file, document cleaning,
summarize by group, join metadata, produce one figure and one
map, and render a Quarto report.
:::

# The full pipeline at a glance

*(To be written.)* A consolidated review of each stage with a
pointer back to the chapter that introduced it.

# End-to-end case study

*(To be written.)* A single worked example threading the whole
workflow on one open dataset.

# Final exam preparation

*(To be written.)* The Guided-Answer debugging ladder — read the
error, identify the object / function / data step, inspect the
data, reduce to a minimal example, test one fix, document the
verification — and a sample exam walkthrough.

# Where to go next

*(To be written.)* Future learning paths: statistical modelling,
spatial data science, time series, and reproducible research.
